_provenance.thesis: Jeswani, K.K. (2021). The Seismic Resilience of Critical Spatially-Distributed Building Portfolios. MASc Thesis, University of Toronto.
_provenance.methodology: Almufti & Willford (2013). REDi Rating System v1.0. Arup.
notes: All delay distributions are lognormal (theta=median in days, beta=log-standard deviation).
Units: days. YAML source uses days for inspection and weeks for all others; converted to days here (1 week = 7 days).
Default values correspond to the PRIMARY SCHOOL ARCHETYPES group (C1-M, C1-L, PTC1-M, CWS-L, C1-H, S1-M) which cover the majority of the Jeswani (2021) portfolio.
Repair-class-dependent variants are captured in sub-categories below and in comments; defaults use Max Repair Class = 3 (the binding/conservative case for mixed portfolios).

PERMITTING vs ENGINEER split: Table 6-7 combines engineering mobilization + permitting as a single delay path. The split below (approx. 50/50 of combined median) is engineering judgment. LOW CONFIDENCE — flag for Kevin's review.

LONG-LEAD MATERIALS: No standalone theta/beta in Table 6-7. Glazing/curtain wall marked '(LL)' in Table D-16 but no days given. Value below adopted from REDi v1.0 general knowledge (14 days median, beta=0.50). LOW CONFIDENCE — verify against Almufti & Willford 2013 App. A4.3.
provenance_confidence: inspection=HIGH, financing=HIGH, contractor=HIGH, engineer_combined=HIGH, permitting=LOW (split is estimated), long_lead=LOW (no thesis value)

_inspection_note: Non-Essential Facility category. theta=5 days, beta=0.54. Source: Table 6-7 (p.135). Applies to all archetypes. provenance_confidence: HIGH.
_financing_note: SBA-Backed Loans category for primary school archetypes. theta=48 weeks * 7 = 336 days, beta=0.57. Source: Table 6-7 (p.135). provenance_confidence: HIGH. Alternate values in YAML: simple_non_engineered=6wks/42days/beta=1.11, wood_frame=15wks/105days/beta=0.68.
_permitting_note: ENGINEERING JUDGMENT SPLIT. Table 6-7 gives combined 'engineer mobilization + permitting' as 4 weeks (28 days, beta=0.54) for Max Repair Class 3. Permitting estimated at ~50% of combined = 14 days; engineer_median_days takes remaining 14 days. LOW CONFIDENCE. provenance_confidence: LOW.
_contractor_note: Non-essential facility <20 stories, Max Repair Class = 3. theta=19 weeks * 7 = 133 days, beta=0.38. Source: Table 6-7 (p.135). provenance_confidence: HIGH. Alternate (Max RC=1): theta=7wks=49days, beta=0.60.
_engineer_note: ENGINEERING JUDGMENT SPLIT. Combined 'engineer mobilization + permitting' per Table 6-7 = 4 weeks (28 days, beta=0.54) for Max Repair Class 3. Engineer share estimated at ~50% = 14 days; permitting takes remaining 14 days. LOW CONFIDENCE. Alternate (Max RC=1): combined=2wks=14days/beta=0.32; (complete redesign): combined=42wks=294days/beta=0.45. provenance_confidence: LOW.
_long_lead_note: NO THESIS VALUE. Glazing/curtain wall components flagged as Long Lead (LL) in Table D-16 but no days or beta given. Value of 14 days median / beta=0.50 adopted from REDi v1.0 general knowledge. LOW CONFIDENCE — must verify against Almufti & Willford 2013 App. A4.3 or thesis Appendix D. provenance_confidence: LOW.

_sub_categories._note: Sub-category values for reference only; not loaded by ImpedingFactorParams. Use for future archetype-specific calibration.
